data.title: Supply@ME Issues New Shares After Warrant Exercise, Updates Share Capital
data.description:
Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Supply@ME ( (GB:SYME) ) has issued an update.

Supply@ME Capital has reported the exercise of 75,044 open offer warrants by qualifying shareholders, triggering the issuance of an equal number of new ordinary shares and raising £48.78 in proceeds. The company has applied for these shares to be admitted to trading on the London Stock Exchange’s main market, with admission expected around 13 April 2026.

Following the admission of the new shares, Supply@ME’s issued share capital will rise to 71,732,714,450 ordinary shares, while 160,200,497 open offer warrants will remain outstanding. The updated share count provides the new denominator for investors’ disclosure calculations under FCA transparency rules, slightly adjusting the company’s capital structure but leaving its broader inventory monetisation strategy unchanged.

More about Supply@ME

Supply@ME Capital is a fintech business focused on an innovative platform that enables manufacturing and trading companies to monetise their inventory to generate cash flow. Its non-credit, non-debt model allows eligible warehouse stock and goods in import or export transactions to be sold to third-party funders, providing liquidity without traditional borrowing.

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: £2.15M

data.description:

An update from Naked Wines plc ( (GB:WINE) ) is now available.

Naked Wines plc has continued to execute its recently announced share buyback programme, repurchasing 25,000 ordinary shares at 73.9 pence each through broker Panmure Liberum. Following this latest transaction, the company has 71,717,079 ordinary shares in issue, of which 4,213,165 are held in treasury, leaving 67,503,914 shares carrying voting rights.

The updated share count and voting-rights figure provide investors with an accurate denominator for regulatory disclosure thresholds under the FCA’s transparency rules. The ongoing buyback signals continued capital management efforts, which may influence earnings per share metrics and the ownership dynamics for existing shareholders as the programme progresses.

The most recent analyst rating on (GB:WINE) stock is a Hold with a £64.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Naked Wines plc stock, see the GB:WINE Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on WINE Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, WINE is a Neutral.

Naked Wines plc’s overall stock score reflects a challenging financial performance with declining revenue and profitability. However, the recent earnings call provided positive insights into cash generation and strategic improvements, which partially offset the negative valuation metrics. Technical indicators suggest a neutral market sentiment.

To see Spark’s full report on WINE stock, click here.

More about Naked Wines plc

Naked Wines plc is an online wine retailer that operates an inclusive wine club model across the UK, U.S. and Australia, connecting its customers, known as Angels, directly with more than 300 independent winemakers. Founded in 2008, the company funds winemakers’ production costs upfront to secure a broad range of over 2,500 wines from 23 countries, offering quality, choice and personalised recommendations while passing cost savings to members.

Horizon Minerals Ltd ( (AU:HRZ) ) has shared an announcement.

Maritana Minerals Limited, previously known as Horizon Minerals Limited, has rebranded following shareholder approval, adopting a new corporate name while retaining its existing registered office, principal place of business and Australian Business Number. The miner’s securities will begin trading under the new ASX ticker MRT from 13 April 2026, signaling a fresh phase of growth and market positioning under the updated brand without altering its underlying operations.

The change of name and code underscores the board’s push to refresh the company’s identity as it enters its next stage of development, a move likely aimed at sharpening its profile with investors in the competitive Australian resources sector. With core operational details unchanged, stakeholders can expect continuity in day‑to‑day activities, while the branding shift may help differentiate the company and support future capital markets engagement.

More about Horizon Minerals Ltd

Maritana Minerals Limited, formerly Horizon Minerals Limited, operates in the minerals and mining sector and is listed on the Australian Securities Exchange. The company focuses on resource exploration and development in Western Australia, maintaining its existing business locations and corporate identifiers aside from its new trading name and ticker code.

Yield Go Holdings Ltd. ( (HK:1796) ) has provided an announcement.

Metaspacex Limited has requested a halt in trading of its shares on the Stock Exchange of Hong Kong, effective from 9:00 a.m. on April 10, 2026. The suspension comes as the company prepares to issue an announcement regarding a reduction in the controlling shareholder’s stake, which has been classified as inside information and may signal a shift in ownership dynamics and governance considerations for investors.

The company has not yet disclosed detailed terms or reasons behind the controlling shareholder’s planned divestment, leaving the market awaiting clarity on potential changes in influence or strategic direction. The trading halt aims to ensure an orderly market and equal access to material information once the announcement is released, reflecting regulatory focus on transparency and investor protection in Hong Kong’s equity markets.

data.title: Sanyo Trading to Acquire Kitakyu Sensui, Expanding Marine and Offshore Wind Business
data.description:
Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Sanyo Trading Co., Ltd. ( (JP:3176) ) just unveiled an update.

Sanyo Trading Co., Ltd. has decided to acquire all shares of Kitakyu Sensui Co., Ltd., a Kyushu-based provider of commercial diving services and related equipment, turning it into a wholly owned subsidiary. Kitakyu Sensui brings more than 40 years of experience in oceanographic surveys, inspections, construction work and sales of diving equipment and high-pressure compressors, with steady revenues and profits.

By integrating Kitakyu Sensui, which will first absorb the Kyushu Diving Center business operated by its founder, Sanyo Trading aims to expand its ocean-related value chain and provide combined solutions spanning materials, equipment and underwater services. The deal is also intended to support the company’s push into offshore wind power generation-related business, reinforcing its long-term growth strategy and positioning in marine infrastructure and renewable energy markets.

The most recent analyst rating on (JP:3176) stock is a Buy with a Yen1992.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Sanyo Trading Co., Ltd. stock, see the JP:3176 Stock Forecast page.

More about Sanyo Trading Co., Ltd.

Sanyo Trading Co., Ltd. is a Japan-based trading company listed on the TSE Prime Market, focusing on materials, equipment and solution-based services across industrial segments. The group is pursuing a long-term growth strategy under its Sanyo Vision 2028 plan, emphasizing the nurturing of core businesses, mergers and acquisitions, and strategic investments to broaden its value chain and enhance its presence in specialized markets such as ocean-related industries and renewable energy.
